name: White Sauce/Bechamel (#10)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, melt the butter over low heat until foamy.
With a wooden spoon, stir in the flour until a thick paste forms. This is called a "roux."
2. Whisk in the milk until smooth. Stirring occasionally, cook 10 to 12 minutes until simmering, thick, and smooth. Simmer for an additional 2 minutes to cook out the flour taste.
3. Season with the salt and freshly ground black pepper.
TIP: If you brown the butter slightly after it has melted (without burning it), it will add a nutty flavor to the sauce.
